user_query:
I’m preparing a peer review of a colleague’s draft on ancient drinking customs, and I also want to stage the review on my notes site. Please complete the following using only the files in this workspace, and produce the specified artifacts.

Inputs you must use:
- input/manuscripts/article_draft.md
- input/assets/manifest.csv
- input/site/mkdocs.yml
- input/docs/index.md
- input/email_draft_raw.txt

Deliverables and requirements:
1) Peer review document (for the site):
   - Create input/docs/reviews/ancient_drinking_customs_peer_review.md with these sections, clearly labeled:
     - Summary (<=150 words): concise overview of the draft’s argument.
     - Contextualization: 2–3 bullet points that broaden the cultural context (e.g., contrasting Greek symposia and Roman convivium, ritual/economic dimensions) as it relates to the draft.
     - Major comments: at least 3 numbered critiques tied to specific evidence in the draft. For each comment, include one exact sentence quoted from input/manuscripts/article_draft.md (copy the sentence verbatim) and then a “Proposed rewrite:” line that improves tone/clarity while preserving the author’s intent.
     - Minor issues: list any broken or inconsistent paths/filenames you find by comparing references in the draft to input/assets/manifest.csv. Include the exact incorrect path from the draft and the corrected path taken from manifest.csv.
   - The review must specifically address the broader cultural significance of drinking customs (as a historian would) and must include exactly two sentence-level rewrites (see next item) that you will also apply in the corrected copies below.

2) Corrected excerpts page (for the site):
   - Create input/docs/excerpts/corrected_snippets.md containing:
     - The corrected figure line from the draft with the image path fixed to the rel_path specified in input/assets/manifest.csv.
     - The two rewritten sentences you proposed in the peer review’s Major comments section (label each “Rewritten sentence 1/2”).

3) Minimal corrected copy of the article:
   - Create output/manuscripts/article_draft_with_corrections.md by copying input/manuscripts/article_draft.md and making only these changes:
     - Fix the figure path so it matches the rel_path in input/assets/manifest.csv for krater.jpg.
     - Replace exactly the same two sentences you rewrote in the peer review with your rewritten versions. Do not change any other text.

4) Site configuration update (essential: inspect and modify configuration):
   - Update input/site/mkdocs.yml to add two navigation entries under nav:
     - A group or item for “Peer Reviews” linking to reviews/ancient_drinking_customs_peer_review.md.
     - A group or item for “Excerpts” linking to excerpts/corrected_snippets.md.
   - Save a backup of the modified configuration to output/site/mkdocs.yml.bak (the backup must reflect the updated nav so we can verify changes without opening the original).

5) Professional communications (essential: draft and rewrite messages):
   - Rewrite input/email_draft_raw.txt for professional tone and brevity, and save the result to output/emails/colleague_email.txt. The email should:
     - Open courteously and summarize what you’re sending.
     - Reference the peer review and corrected copy by their exact paths: input/docs/reviews/ancient_drinking_customs_peer_review.md and output/manuscripts/article_draft_with_corrections.md.
     - Mention the corrected image path and that the two sentences were revised for clarity.
     - Keep to 120–180 words.
   - Draft a brief message to the departmental editor summarizing changes to the site nav, saved to output/messages/editor_message.txt (3–5 bullet points, each mentioning the added nav items and their target paths).

Key constraints:
- Do not use the internet. Ground all checks in the provided files. Confirm the correct image path using input/assets/manifest.csv (krater.jpg should match its rel_path there).
- Ensure the two rewritten sentences in the peer review exactly correspond to two sentences present in input/manuscripts/article_draft.md; include the originals verbatim in the peer review and apply the rewritten versions in both the excerpts page and the minimal corrected copy.
- Make new directories as needed, but use the exact output paths specified above.

I will consider this complete when all listed files exist with the required content, and input/site/mkdocs.yml has the new nav entries. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
